tools.name: find_attack_techniques
tools.title: Find Attack Techniques
tools.description: List every YARA detection on the memory image, sorted by severity, with their MITRE ATT&CK technique. Use this first to answer 'is this machine compromised?' — it gives the overview of the detected kill-chain.

examples.name: Threat overview
examples.description: All APT detections ranked by criticality
examples.expected_use: First pivot of a forensic investigation

execution.template: search index=forensics sourcetype=forensics:yara_hit | eval sev_rank=case(severity="critical",1,severity="high",2,severity="medium",3,severity="informational",5,1=1,4) | sort sev_rank | table signature, severity, mitre, description

tools.name: investigate_process
tools.title: Investigate Process
tools.description: Search one or more processes by name (e.g. powershell.exe, cmd.exe, ntdsutil.exe) in the domain controller memory image. Returns PID, PPID, creation time, session. Use it to pivot from a YARA detection to the responsible process.

process_name.description: Process name to investigate (e.g. powershell.exe). SPL wildcards are supported (e.g. *.exe).

execution.template: search index=forensics sourcetype=forensics:process process_name=$process_name$ | table create_time, process_id, parent_process_id, process_name, session_id, threads | sort create_time

tools.name: attack_timeline
tools.title: Attack Timeline
tools.description: Reconstruct the attack timeline by merging processes and YARA detections on a single time axis. Each row carries the real timestamp, the artifact (rule or process), the MITRE technique and the severity. Use it to tell the incident story.

examples.name: Incident timeline
examples.description: Unified time view of processes + detections
examples.expected_use: Build the attack narrative for the report

execution.template: search index=forensics | eval artifact=coalesce(signature, process_name), kind=if(sourcetype="forensics:yara_hit","detection","process") | table _time, kind, artifact, mitre, severity, process_id, parent_process_id | sort _time

tools.name: triage_summary
tools.title: Triage Summary
tools.description: Triage summary: number of detections by severity and by MITRE ATT&CK technique. Gives a quick read on the scope and nature of the compromise. Use it to prioritize the response.

examples.name: Prioritization
examples.description: Count detections by severity and technique

execution.template: search index=forensics sourcetype=forensics:yara_hit | stats count as detections, values(signature) as signatures by severity, mitre | sort -detections

tools.name: ai_triage
tools.title: AI Triage (| ai)
tools.description: Splunk-native AI triage: aggregates the YARA detections from the forensics index and has Claude analyze them via the `| ai` command of the Splunk AI Toolkit. Returns a compromise verdict, the summarized MITRE ATT&CK kill-chain and remediation recommendations. Use it to get an AI-reasoned incident summary directly from Splunk.

examples.name: AI incident summary
examples.description: Verdict + kill-chain + remediation generated by Claude via | ai

execution.template: search index=forensics sourcetype=forensics:yara_hit | eval line=severity.": ".signature." (".mitre.")" | stats list(line) as dets | eval detections=mvjoin(dets, " | ") | fields detections | ai connection="claude" prompt="You are a senior forensic analyst. Here are the YARA detections on a compromised Windows domain controller: {detections}. Give a concise answer in English: 1) a verdict (COMPROMISED/SUSPICIOUS/CLEAN), 2) a summary of the MITRE ATT&CK kill-chain, 3) three remediation recommendations."
